text: Welcome back to the show. Today we're talking with Maria Chen about the future of large language models.
Maria Chen has spent the last decade building infrastructure for distributed training at scale.
So let's just dive right in.

One thing I keep coming back to is how much compute costs have fallen. Five years ago this conversation would have looked completely different. Now a small team can train something respectable on a modest budget.
Switching gears, let's talk about sourdough starters and why mine keeps dying every winter.
Honestly, keeping a starter alive in a cold apartment is its own science experiment.
Before we wrap up, John Kim from the audience sent in a question about whether Maria Chen and John Kim will collaborate on an open dataset next year.
